action.reasoning: Observation: The evaluation failed because `/opt/py312/bin/python` lacks `mediapy`, while `/usr/bin/python` lacks `lerobot`. The environment is fragmented in both the sandbox and the eval container. Analysis: I can bypass this fragmentation completely by using a client-server architecture. `predict.py` will run in the default `/usr/bin/python` (which has `VLABench`, `mediapy`, `mujoco`) and spawn a `policy_server.py` process using `/opt/py312/bin/python` (which has `lerobot`, `torch`). They will communicate via `stdin`/`stdout` and `/tmp` files for fast IPC. Decision: Implement `policy_server.py`, update `predict.py` to use it, revert `train.py` to the working version from iteration 27 (removing `HF_HOME` which caused the cache error), and submit the evaluation.
